steps.title: Identify what both texts establish
steps.content:
Text 1 provides evidence: the northern pike will face worse outcomes and require conservation efforts, while the white-tailed deer can withstand warming without needing conservation.
Text 2 provides the allocation principle: agencies should focus conservation efforts on species that are most threatened and most in need of help.

steps.title: Apply the principle to the evidence
steps.content:
Choice B applies Text 2's principle to Text 1's evidence, concluding that since the northern pike is most threatened, agencies should prioritize its conservation over the white-tailed deer.
The other three either contradict the evidence (D falsely extends conservation to both species despite Text 1 saying the deer won't need conservation) or assert unsupported claims (C claims collaboration improves outcomes; A addresses preventing temperature increases, outside both texts' scope).
That matches choice B — B is correct.
